evals.prompt: Run a repo audit on a project called 'taskflow'. README.md states: 'A minimal CLI for tracking tasks. Just two commands: start and done.' The CLI source has 12 subcommands including export, sync, import, archive, and undo. No design doc exists.
evals.expected_output: The skill produces a structured report covering all 4 dimensions with ratings, identifies the philosophy-implementation gap (claims minimal but has 12 commands), notes the missing design doc, and writes the report to critique/canon-critique.md.
expectations: Report includes all four dimension headings with ratings
Dimension 2 flags the contradiction between 'two commands' claim and 12 actual subcommands
Dimension 2 notes the missing philosophy/design doc as a finding
Describes writing the report to critique/canon-critique.md
Report starts with 'Audit run: MM-DD-YYYY hh:mm AM/PM'

evals.prompt: Run a repo audit. After composing the full report, write it to the correct output file.
evals.expected_output: The skill writes the report to critique/canon-critique.md in the repo under analysis, creating the critique/ directory if needed, replacing any prior contents.
expectations: Describes writing or would write the report to critique/canon-critique.md
Replaces prior contents rather than appending
The first line of the written file is the audit DateTime

evals.prompt: Run a repo audit on a project with no docs/how-it-works.md, no DESIGN.md, no ARCHITECTURE.md, and no ADRs. README.md states a philosophy but no dedicated doc exists.
evals.expected_output: The skill extracts the philosophy from README.md for Dimension 2, notes the absence of a dedicated philosophy/design doc as a finding, and completes the audit without stopping.
expectations: Extracts stated philosophy from README.md for Dimension 2
Notes the absence of a dedicated philosophy or design doc as a finding
Completes the audit across all four dimensions
Does not stop or error because the design doc is missing

evals.prompt: During a repo audit you find a DRY violation: the same database query appears in three separate files. Should you fix it?
evals.expected_output: The skill reports the violation in Dimension 4 with file:line references and a fix recommendation, but does not modify any files. Fixing is outside the scope of repo-audit.
expectations: Reports the DRY violation in Dimension 4 with file:line format
Does not modify any source files
Provides a recommendation sentence without implementing it
